text.title: performance-domains-overview Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for performance-domains-overview as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: The 8 domains overlap heavily; teams waste time arguing where an issue "belongs."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: That is the point. Issues usually cascade across domains. Use domain overlap to reveal root causes.

failure-mode.description: Domain colors (green/yellow/red) collapse multi-dimensional reality into a bumper sticker.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Colors are a snapshot. Pair with evidence quotes and trend arrows (is this domain improving or degrading?). Use sparklines to track direction.

failure-mode.description: Lacks prescribed cadence — easy to do the assessment once and never repeat.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Schedule monthly assessments (the same day each month) AND ad-hoc trigger assessments after major milestones or incidents.

failure-mode.description: Doesn't say which domain to fix first when several are red.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use domain interaction knowledge to identify the domain that, if improved, unblocks the most others. Often Uncertainty (risks) or Team (morale/engagement).

failure-mode.description: Agents over-rate "Team" and "Stakeholder" green because retros are usually polite — counterweight with attrition / NPS data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Retros capture team sentiment in the moment. Attrition and NPS lag but are more reliable signals.

failure-mode.description: Don't let a single agent both score AND prescribe actions in one pass; bias compounds. Split into two prompts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Scoring agents are subject to anchoring bias. Separate assessment from recommendations to reduce compounding.

failure-mode.description: Force evidence-per-cell; without it, the agent produces a beige consensus assessment.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Evidence forces specificity. Agents without evidence anchors regress to "everything is yellow."

failure-mode.description: Watch for stale data: assessments must declare the time window of evidence consulted.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A green domain from January may be red now. Explicit time window prevents false positives.

failure-mode.description: Domain interactions table is high-value; agents tend to forget cross-domain cause/effect — prompt for it explicitly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents default to siloed thinking. Explicit interaction prompting (e.g., "how do risks cascade to team morale?") yields better root causes.
